items.caption: A schematic diagram illustrating the main components of a biosensor. it features a black box labeled "s" connected to a black box labeled "p" by a black arrow. from "p," there is a pink arrow pointing to a black box labeled "reference," which is connected to a black box labeled "d" by a black arrow. from "d," there is a black arrow pointing to a black box labeled "e," which is connected to a black box labeled "2.376" by a black arrow. the background is a light blue with a subtle gradient, and there is a text overlay at the top of the image that reads "what are biosensors?" followed by a sentence explaining that a biosensor is an analytical device that converts a biological response into an electrical signal. below the diagram, there is additional text that states, "schematic diagram showing the main components of a biosensor. the biochemist (a) converts the substrate to product. this reaction is determined by the transducer (b) which converts it to an electrical signal. the output from the transducer is amplified (c), processed (d), and displayed (e)."
